evals.prompt: Black Friday just started — we’re 3 hours in. Sessions are 3x normal but orders are only 1.2x. ROAS is below our breakeven. What should we do with ads and the site?
evals.expected_output: Traffic → efficiency → hold/cut ads + landing/checkout checklist; breakeven framing.
expectations: Follows or states steps: traffic, conversion efficiency, branch on high vs low efficiency
Addresses first hours of promo and ROAS below breakeven
Recommends not scaling when conversion is weak; suggests landing/checkout checks
Includes a table or matrix (efficiency snapshot and/or action matrix)
Mentions dynamic ad adjustment (pause, hold, or fix before spend)

evals.prompt: Our hero dress SKU sold out in 20 minutes but ads still run to that PDP. CVR on that page tanked. Another color is in stock. How do we reallocate?
evals.expected_output: Pause OOS ads, redirect traffic, restock note, allocation table.
expectations: Addresses instant sellout and ads still pointing to OOS
Recommends pausing or redirecting traffic to in-stock variant or category
Includes restock or traffic allocation guidance
Ties CVR drop to OOS or UX
Efficiency or action framing consistent with promo stress monitor

evals.prompt: First 4 hours of our sale: ROAS is 4x breakeven, nothing OOS, server stable. We have budget left. What’s the move?
evals.expected_output: Scale or reallocate up; efficiency high branch.
expectations: Recommends scaling or increasing spend on winners when ROAS above breakeven
References real-time or early-window traffic and conversion efficiency
Mentions cautions (stock, frequency cap) if appropriate
Does not only say check landing page — must endorse scaling or increasing spend
